channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: JD Vance holds peace talks with Iran as Trump threatens strikes over Hormuz
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina, Sammy Westfall, Susannah George
item.description: The president's warning underscores persistent tensions even as Vance said in Switzerland that he hoped the two sides would “turn over a new leaf” in their relationship.

item.title: For cash-strapped farmers, deal to end Iran fighting comes too late
item.dc:creator: David J. Lynch
item.description: Some of the president’s strongest supporters are hurting as midterm elections approach.

item.title: Chaotic run-up to U.S.-Iran talks signals steep hurdles ahead
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix
item.description: Neither Washington nor Tehran wants the peace process to collapse completely, but diplomats say they expect a process full of challenges and distrust.

item.title: Trump said Italy’s prime minister ‘begged’ for a photo. She says that’s false.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola, Stefano Pitrelli
item.description: Italy’s right-wing prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, was labeled Europe’s “Trump whisperer,” but the war in Iran turned their friendship into a liability for her.

item.title: War-weary Iranians see glimmers of relief as Trump deal takes shape
item.dc:creator: Susannah George, Asal Abasian
item.description: As details emerged of an initial agreement between Washington and Tehran, Iranians voiced muted hopes for peace and prosperity.

item.title: Shut out of the World Cup again, China roots for a referee
item.dc:creator: Huiyee Chiew
item.description: On-field officials typically strive for anonymity. Ma Ning — or Card Master back home — has taken a different approach.
